skill.name: context-optimization
skill.description: Guides AI agents through context compaction, observation masking, KV-cache friendly prompt design, and context partitioning for long-running work.
skill.summary: Optimize AI context usage with compaction, masking, cache-aware prompts, and partitioning guidance.
skill.author: muratcankoylan

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found that most are false positives from Markdown code fences, conceptual references, and example text. The skill includes a Python utility script and one non-security MD5 reference ID generator, so publication is reasonable with low risk awareness.

low_findings.title: Non-Security MD5 Reference ID Generation
low_findings.description: The script imports hashlib and uses hashlib.md5 to create short in-memory observation reference IDs. This confirms the weak-cryptography pattern, but the value is not used for authentication, signatures, password storage, or secret protection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The MD5 call is directly present, but surrounding code shows it only generates a local reference key. No evidence found that it protects secrets or crosses a trust boundary.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings point to fenced Python examples and explanatory snippets. No evidence found of subprocess, shell execution, backtick command execution, or user-controlled command construction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited regions are Markdown examples or plain documentation. The executable script was reviewed separately and does not invoke shell commands.

low_findings.title: Network and Reconnaissance Terms Are Conceptual References
low_findings.description: The network and reconnaissance findings are caused by terms such as requests, system prompt, memory retrieval, and cache metrics. No evidence found of HTTP clients, sockets, host discovery, credential collection, or outbound data transfer.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed code stores data in memory and calculates local metrics only. No import or call for network communication was found in the cited files.

content.value_statement: Long AI sessions can become slow, costly, and less accurate when context grows without structure. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical methods to reduce token use while preserving important task state.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to compact context based on token utilization and quality signals.
Provides selection rules for masking old or verbose tool observations.
Shows how to order stable prompt content for better KV-cache reuse.
Guides task partitioning across focused agent contexts.
Includes Python utilities for token estimation, masking, budgets, and cache metrics.
Describes monitoring targets for token reduction, latency, quality, and cost.
limitations: Token counting uses rough character-based estimates unless integrated with a model tokenizer.
Summarization examples use simple heuristics and are not production-grade by themselves.
The skill provides guidance and utilities, not a complete hosted optimization service.
Cache benefits depend on the model provider and infrastructure support.

use_cases.title: Reduce Long-Session Token Costs
use_cases.description: Apply compaction and masking when repeated tool outputs and old turns consume most of the context budget.

use_cases.title: Design Cache-Friendly Agent Prompts
use_cases.description: Place stable prompt sections first and isolate dynamic values to improve prefix reuse across similar requests.

use_cases.title: Plan Multi-Agent Context Partitioning
use_cases.description: Split large research or implementation work into focused subcontexts and aggregate concise results.

prompt_templates.title: Audit My Context Usage
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a long session or prompt design.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the context-optimization skill to review this workflow. Identify what should stay, what can be summarized, and what can be masked.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Token Budget
prompt_templates.scenario: Plan context allocation before building an agent workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the context-optimization skill to create a token budget for this agent. Allocate space for system prompt, tools, retrieved documents, history, and buffer.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Cache Stability
prompt_templates.scenario: Refactor repeated prompts for lower latency and cost.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the context-optimization skill to make this prompt more KV-cache friendly. Keep stable sections first and move dynamic values later.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Partitioned Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced decomposition for large tasks that exceed one context.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the context-optimization skill to partition this task across sub-agents. Define subtask boundaries, retained context, summaries, and aggregation rules.

output_examples.input: A support analysis agent keeps full logs, repeated search results, and every prior message in context.
output: Keep the current ticket, user constraints, and latest findings in full.
Summarize older conversation turns into decisions, open questions, and commitments.
Mask repeated logs behind references with short keys and retrieval notes.

output_examples.input: A batch evaluation workflow sends a different timestamp at the top of every request.
output: Move timestamps out of the stable prefix.
Place system instructions, tool definitions, and rubric text before variable content.
Track cache hit rate before and after the prompt change.

output_examples.input: A research task needs more documents than the model context can hold.
output: Partition by source group or research question.
Have each focused worker return claims, evidence quality, and unresolved gaps.
Aggregate only validated summaries into the coordinator context.

best_practices: Measure context usage and quality before adding optimization machinery.
Preserve current goals, user preferences, recent turns, and unresolved decisions during compaction.
Test token savings together with answer quality, latency, and retrieval reliability.
anti_patterns: Do not compact system instructions or active user requirements.
Do not mask observations that are still needed for current reasoning.
Do not add dynamic timestamps or session identifiers to reusable prompt prefixes.

faq.question: Does this skill increase the model context window?
faq.answer: No. It helps use the available context more effectively through compression, masking, caching, and partitioning.

faq.question: Can it reduce API cost?
faq.answer: Yes, when long prompts or repeated context dominate cost. Results depend on workload, model pricing, and cache support.

faq.question: Does it require a specific model provider?
faq.answer: No. The concepts are provider-neutral, but tokenizer accuracy and cache behavior vary by provider.

faq.question: Is the included Python script production ready?
faq.answer: No. It is a utility example with heuristic token counts and simple summarization logic.

faq.question: When should context compaction trigger?
faq.answer: The skill suggests monitoring around 70 percent utilization and triggering stronger action near 80 percent.

faq.question: How is observation masking different from deletion?
faq.answer: Masking replaces verbose content with a compact reference so important details can be retrieved when needed.
